title: NASA to select new headquarters building by end of year
summary: NASA intends to choose a new headquarters building by the end of the year, though it will stay within the Washington, D.C. area. The decision aims to modernize its workspace while maintaining proximity to key operations.

title: Rounding up the space unicorns
summary: Billion-dollar space startups, once rare, are now growing rapidly across emerging orbital markets. This trend highlights the increasing commercialization and investment in the space industry.

title: NASA interested in Hubble reboost if costs can be reduced
summary: NASA is considering a reboost mission for the Hubble Space Telescope if operating costs can be reduced. This follows plans to reboost another spacecraft in a decaying orbit.

title: NASA concerns about Russian repairs prompted ISS safe haven decision
summary: NASA instructed ISS astronauts to take shelter in a Crew Dragon spacecraft due to concerns over Russian cosmonauts' repairs, which were deemed to pose an 'elevated risk' to the station.

title: Leaf Space partners with D-Orbit and EnduroSat to test connectivity service
summary: Leaf Space has partnered with D-Orbit and EnduroSat to test a new space connectivity service called TreeNet, which aims to improve seamless space communications. The service was unveiled on May 27 by the Italian ground segment operator.

title: Astronauts briefly shelter in Dragon during ISS leak repair
summary: Astronauts on the International Space Station were instructed to shelter in a Dragon spacecraft while cosmonauts repaired an air leak in a Russian module on June 5. The incident highlights ongoing maintenance challenges aboard the ISS.

title: Next Ariane 6 launch to carry 36 Amazon Leo satellites using upgraded boosters
summary: The next Ariane 6 launch will carry 36 Amazon LEO satellites using upgraded boosters. This marks Amazon's largest satellite deployment to date.

title: Let’s build the moon base, but not lose sight of Mars
summary: NASA and the space industry are experiencing significant advancements, highlighted by the Artemis 2 crew's successful lunar flyby. The focus remains on building a moon base while maintaining Mars as a long-term goal.

title: Qianfan constellation deployment hits 200 satellites with Long March 8 and 6A launches
summary: The Qianfan constellation, led by Shanghai, reached 200 satellites with recent Long March 8 and 6A launches. This follows two experimental flights and accelerates the constellation's deployment.

title: Satellite maker Apex’s valuation rises to $2.3 billion after latest $200 million raise
summary: Apex, a satellite manufacturer, raised $200 million in a funding round, nearly doubling its valuation to $2.3 billion. The company plans to expand its in-house satellite production capabilities with the new funds.

title: Trouble near the Milky Way: The Large Magellanic Cloud is ripping its smaller neighbor galaxy apart
summary: The Large Magellanic Cloud is disrupting its smaller neighbor galaxy due to gravitational interactions. This pair of dwarf galaxies has been interacting for billions of years as they pass near the Milky Way.

title: 10 best Spanish beaches to see the total solar eclipse 2026
summary: On August 12, 2026, some of the world's best beaches in Spain will experience a total solar eclipse. This event is highlighted as a prime viewing opportunity for astronomy enthusiasts.

title: 'Rick and Morty' creators on their inspirations as season 9 tackles the multiverse, evolution, and a kung-fu fight in a Trader Joe's parking lot (interview)
summary: The creators of 'Rick and Morty' discuss their inspirations for season 9, which explores themes like the multiverse, evolution, and a unique kung-fu fight in a Trader Joe's parking lot. They describe the season as a celebration, reflecting their creative vision.

title: Could meteor storms harm NASA's future moon missions?
summary: NASA may delay future moon missions if a major meteor storm is forecasted during crew activity. The crew would remain inside the spacecraft until the storm passes.

title: Who is Lobo, the cigar-smoking alien bounty hunter in 'Supergirl'?
summary: Jason Momoa will star as Lobo, a cigar-smoking alien bounty hunter, in the upcoming 'Supergirl' series. Lobo is a well-known DC Comics character known for his violent and rebellious nature.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 213 — Live From ISDC With Gerry Griffin
summary: The 213th episode of the 'This Week In Space' podcast featured a live interview with Apollo legend Gerry Griffin at the International Space Development Conference (ISDC) in McLean, Virginia.

title: Neil deGrasse Tyson narrates new trailer for upcoming Broadway musical, 'Galileo'
summary: Neil deGrasse Tyson narrates a new trailer for the upcoming Broadway musical 'Galileo', featuring Raúl Esparza in the lead role as the Renaissance astronomer. The musical is set to premiere this fall.

title: I resisted smart telescopes for years — then one changed my life
summary: A personal account discusses the benefits of smart telescopes for stargazing, highlighting their ease of use and the ideal conditions of spring for deep-sky observation.

title: Going supersonic! NASA's X-59 jet breaks sound barrier for the 1st time
summary: NASA's X-59 jet successfully broke the sound barrier for the first time on June 5, achieving a top speed of approximately Mach 1.1. This milestone marks a significant step in supersonic flight research.

title: NASA’s X-59 Aircraft Flies Supersonic for First Time
summary: NASA's X-59 experimental aircraft achieved supersonic flight for the first time on June 5, reaching Mach 1.1 during an 81-minute test flight. The milestone paves the way for demonstrating its quiet supersonic capabilities later in the year.

title: NASA Announces Winners of 2026 University Innovation Competition
summary: NASA announced MIT's project 'Exploration-Class Lunar Integrated Power SystEm' as the winner of the 2026 RASC-AL competition. Two other teams from MIT and Virginia Tech also secured second and third places with their Mars exploration projects.

title: NASA’s Artemis II Moon Mission Research Continues on Earth
summary: NASA's Artemis II Moon Mission research continues on Earth after the crew's successful splashdown on April 10. Science teams are analyzing data from the test flight to support future deep space human exploration.

title: NASA Concludes Antenna Mishap Investigation, Releases Report
summary: NASA has concluded the investigation into the damage to its Deep Space Station 14 (DSS-14) antenna at the Goldstone Deep Space Communications Complex. The incident has been classified as a Type A mishap due to the total cost of damages.

title: First Steps: America’s Grueling Second Spacewalk
summary: NASA conducted its second spacewalk on June 5, 1966, during the Gemini IX-A mission, with astronaut Gene Cernan performing the extravehicular activity. This followed the historic first spacewalk by Edward H. White during Gemini IV one year prior.

title: Fighting Fire With Fire
summary: Prescribed burns are used in Australia's Northern Territory to reduce the severity of wildfires during the season. This practice helps manage fire-prone ecosystems.

title: NASA Hosts 2026 Review on Advanced Composite Manufacturing
summary: NASA's Hi-Rate Composite Aircraft Manufacturing (HiCAM) project held a 2026 spring review at Langley Research Center, bringing together 150 consortium members to assess progress in advanced composite manufacturing.

title: NASA-Funded Study Shows Wildfire Smoke’s Hidden Ozone Toll
summary: A NASA-funded study reveals that wildfire smoke has significantly increased ground-level ozone pollution across much of the contiguous U.S. over the past decade, posing health risks even in areas far from active fires.

title: Colorful, Chaotic Jupiter
summary: NASA's Juno spacecraft captured a color-enhanced image of Jupiter's northern hemisphere during its 61st close flyby on May 12, 2024. Citizen scientist Gary Eason processed the raw data to enhance the image's color and clarity.

title: A Moonlit Earth as Seen From Artemis II
summary: An astronaut aboard Artemis II captured a unique photograph of Earth from space, highlighting the planet's beauty and its position in the cosmos. The image offers a fresh perspective on humanity's home in the universe.

title: NASA concludes MAVEN mission at Mars, uses data to discover new atmospheric phenomenon
summary: NASA has concluded the MAVEN mission at Mars after a six-month effort to restore communication. The mission's data has led to the discovery of a new atmospheric phenomenon.

title: After New Glenn anomaly, Blue Origin keeps focus on upcoming Blue Moon and Mars missions
summary: Blue Origin remains committed to its upcoming Blue Moon and Mars missions despite the loss of its New Glenn rocket during a hotfire test at Launch Complex 36. The company is focusing on recovery and future objectives.

title: The Exploration Company advances Nyx parachute recovery
summary: The Exploration Company (TEC) has made progress in developing the parachute recovery system for its reusable Nyx spacecraft. This advancement is a key milestone for the spacecraft's reusability and future missions.

title: Space Force evaluating anomaly-related damage, future danger areas
summary: Space Launch Delta 45 (SLD 45) is assessing damage and potential future risks related to an anomaly. The Eastern Range, operated by SLD 45, has been cleared for operations following the evaluation.

title: Artemis III’s remaining solid rocket booster segments shipped from Utah to Florida
summary: The remaining solid rocket booster segments for Artemis III have been transported from Utah to Florida. This marks a key step in preparation for the mission, despite ongoing uncertainties about timelines and landers.

title: Blue Origin initiates LC-36 recovery, targets RTF by end of 2026
summary: Blue Origin has begun inspection, repair, and reconstruction activities at Launch Complex-36, aiming for a return-to-flight (RTF) by the end of 2026.

title: Launch Preview: SpaceX and Chinese rockets to launch internet satellites to LEO
summary: SpaceX and Chinese rockets are set to launch internet satellites to Low Earth Orbit (LEO) this week. Five missions are planned, with SpaceX and Chinese organizations leading the launches.

title: Ancient black hole sheds new light on Webb’s Little Red Dots
summary: An international team used the James Webb Space Telescope to study an ancient black hole, providing new insights into Webb's 'Little Red Dots'. The discovery enhances understanding of early cosmic structures.

title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn explodes during Static Fire test at LC-36
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket suffered a catastrophic explosion during a static fire test at Launch Complex 36. The anomaly occurred during a planned hot-fire test, marking a significant setback for the company's orbital launch ambitions.

title: Rovers and Drones laying the foundations for NASA Moon Base
summary: NASA has outlined initial missions for its Moon Base initiative, leveraging rovers and drones to establish foundational infrastructure. The initiative involves rebranding existing Commercial Lunar Payload Services (CLPS) missions.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website provides industry news related to the space sector. The source covers various aspects of the space industry, including launches, technology, and policy.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's milestones in space exploration and technology.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are contributing to ESA's missions by testing and selecting materials that can withstand the harsh conditions of space. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section is leading this effort with innovative approaches.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance efficiency and accessibility in space missions and outreach.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news discusses the sturdy and sensitive aspects of a space industry component or system. The image accompanying the text highlights these qualities.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical test for spacecraft parachutes, referred to as a 'bake-out' process, to ensure their reliability during re-entry. This involves heating and stress-testing the parachutes to simulate harsh conditions.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA's ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-meter parachute for Mars by sterilizing it to prevent microbial contamination. The parachute will slow the rover's descent during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life on Mars.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing and securing objects in space. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal missions.

title: AstroForge Completes its DeepSpace-2 Spacecraft
summary: AstroForge has completed its DeepSpace-2 spacecraft, which aims to demonstrate the company's capability to rendezvous with an asteroid. This mission sets the foundation for future asteroid mining efforts planned before the end of the decade.

title: Apex Raises $200M At $2.3B Valuation
summary: Apex has raised $200 million in a funding round, valuing the company at $2.3 billion. This is the third time in 14 months that Apex has secured a $200 million investment.

title: US Commercial Space Stations Expand to Europe
summary: The US commercial space stations are expanding their presence into Europe, ensuring continued European access to Low Earth Orbit (LEO) as the International Space Station (ISS) nears the end of its operational life. This expansion highlights growing international collaboration in the commercial space sector.

title: Recapping Planet’s Q1 With CFO and President Ashley Johnson
summary: Planet's Q1 recap highlights its differentiation in revenue recognition compared to hardware-focused companies. CFO and President Ashley Johnson emphasizes Planet's approach to mitigating binary outcomes.

title: The Exploration Company Completes Nyx Drop Test
summary: The Exploration Company completed a Nyx drop test on May 19 to evaluate parachute performance and vehicle dynamics during a controlled Earth landing. The test used a dedicated drop-test vehicle to simulate the capsule's descent.

title: Austria’s First Commercial Sat is Ready for Launch
summary: Austria's first commercial satellite, Oasis Alpha, has been shipped by Vienna-based startup Tumbleweed to launch integrator Exolaunch for a planned July launch. This marks a significant milestone for Austria's space industry.

title: Muon Space Unveils New, Larger Satellite Bus
summary: Muon Space has introduced a new, larger satellite bus named Condor-Ultra, which is three times larger than its predecessor Condor-XL and designed for the emerging orbital-data-center market.

title: NASA Reverts to Original CLD Procurement Plan
summary: NASA has decided to revert to its original Commercial Lunar Payload Services (CLD) procurement plan. This move aims to align with industry feedback and maintain the original commercial strategy.

title: Blue Origin Commits to Return to Flight This Year
summary: Blue Origin has announced plans to resume flight operations by the end of the year after assessing damage to its launch infrastructure. CEO Dave Limp confirmed the return-to-flight commitment on social media.

title: NDAA Would Cut SDA, RCO Offices
summary: The House's annual defense authorization bill proposes eliminating two Pentagon offices, the Space Development Agency (SDA) and the Rapid Capabilities Office (RCO), which are focused on accelerating space acquisition. This move could significantly impact the U.S. military's space capabilities and acquisition strategies.

title: FCC Gives Amazon Leo 50% Deployment Waiver, With Conditions on Spectrum Priority
summary: The FCC granted Amazon a 50% deployment waiver for its Project Kuiper constellation by July, but imposed conditions on spectrum priority. The decision allows Amazon to accelerate its satellite launch timeline with regulatory oversight.

title: Apex Hits $2.3B Valuation With Latest Funding Raise
summary: Apex Space, a satellite bus manufacturer, raised an additional $200 million in funding, increasing its valuation to $2.3 billion. This funding round more than doubles the company's previous valuation.

title: Aalyria to Support Orchestration for NASA SCaN Program
summary: NASA selected Aalyria to support orchestration for its Space Communications and Navigation (SCaN) Program, starting with the PExT demonstration mission. The PExT mission, Polylingual Experimental Terminal, was launched as part of this initiative.

title: Tototheo Global Taps Eutelsat for LEO Service for Maritime
summary: Tototheo Global has partnered with Eutelsat to provide Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite services for maritime applications. The deal expands Eutelsat's reach in the maritime sector through Tototheo Global's distribution network.

title: NGA Awards Planet $22M Luno B Option for Maritime Domain Awareness
summary: The National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency (NGA) awarded Planet Labs a $22 million contract option to provide AI-enabled maritime domain awareness data analytics for U.S. Combatant Commands. This builds on a previous contract initiated last year.

title: Infinite Orbits and Open Cosmos Plan ‘Tom & Jerry’ Space Rendezvous Mission
summary: Infinite Orbits has selected Open Cosmos to develop two satellites named 'Tom & Jerry' for a LEO rendezvous and inspection mission. The mission aims to demonstrate maneuvering and inspection capabilities in Low-Earth Orbit.

title: Mbryonics and NTT to Collaborate on Optical Communications Infrastructure
summary: Mbryonics and NTT have signed a Memorandum of Understanding to collaborate on developing a high-speed optical communications infrastructure for space. The partnership aims to enhance space communications through advanced photonics technology.

title: Muon Space Debuts High-Power ‘Ultra’ Satellite Designed for Orbital Data Centers
summary: Muon Space introduced a new high-power satellite platform called Condor-Ultra, designed for orbital data centers. The first mission is scheduled for 2025.

title: Beyond Gravity Releases New Propulsion Pointing Mechanism
summary: Beyond Gravity has introduced a new ultra-light propulsion pointing mechanism designed for high maneuverability in Low Earth Orbit (LEO) constellations. The two-axis mechanism aims to address challenges in satellite positioning and movement.

title: Applied Aerospace & Defense IPOs to Raise $650M
summary: Applied Aerospace & Defense raised $650 million through an IPO by offering 32.5 million shares on the New York Stock Exchange. The company began trading on Wednesday.
